title: Natural Language Search for Scientific Discovery
abstract: We study how natural language retrieval can improve scientific paper discovery when users express ideas vaguely instead of using exact keywords. Our method combines semantic retrieval and grounded answer generation.

title: Conference-Scale Abstract Retrieval with Local Language Models
abstract: This paper presents a multi-conference retrieval system that indexes thousands of abstracts, ranks relevant papers, and uses a local language model to generate concise grounded responses without fabricating metadata.

title: Designing Simple Interfaces for Academic Search Assistants
abstract: We evaluate lightweight user interfaces for academic assistants and show that clear search controls, upload feedback, and ranked results improve trust and usability.

title: Semantic Matching for Vague Research Queries
abstract: Semantic embeddings allow vague or descriptive user queries to retrieve relevant research papers even when no exact keyword overlap exists. We compare embedding-based retrieval to lexical baselines.
